criterion: Post-transient trajectories remain finite-time bounded and reproducible.
notes: All configured F5 cases report bounded_candidate.

criterion: At least one validated fractional Lyapunov method is applied to every fractional candidate.
notes: Rigorous attempts are documented, but no fractional method is strictly validated and applied per candidate.

criterion: Prefer sign agreement between two valid applicable fractional methods.
notes: Preferred cross-method sign agreement is not available; method limitations are documented.

criterion: Zero-one diagnostic is recorded and interpreted conservatively.
notes: zero_one_regular_candidate in current cases; compatible with regular/inconclusive, not chaos support.

criterion: PSD/FFT does not force a periodic or chaotic interpretation.
notes: spectral_inconclusive; no strong dominant periodic label but not broadband support.

criterion: Method warnings preserve non-smooth and Caputo-memory limitations.
Phase F reports numerical evidence levels under the recorded contract.
notes: The finite-time interpretation is centralized in the freeze summary.

criterion: Hiddenness assessment remains a separate sampled-neighborhood gate.
notes: Phase F reports chaos evidence; candidate_gate reports hiddenness scope.
